findings.what: BASKET-WORKER SHARDING (PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 + raised side-judge semaphore(48)) — the config that ALREADY deadlocked (19/20 threads futex_wait, SIGKILL). Mechanism = nested bounded thread pools. verified_compose.py:2981/2992 opens ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=N) for the compose MAP; each map thread calls verify_fn->NLI POST->acquire_judge_slot on the process-global BoundedSemaphore (judge_concurrency.py:169). The whole compose runs under asyncio.to_thread, which uses the LOOP DEFAULT executor (~min(32,cpu+4)). At 328-basket scale the default-executor threads are all parked holding the compose call while the ThreadPool(48) threads all park in acquire_judge_slot/fut.result -> thread-pool STARVATION, no thread free to run the continuation that releases a slot.
findings.evidence: verified_compose.py:2981,2992 (ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_basket_workers)); judge_concurrency.py:169 shared BoundedSemaphore + :31-37 de-storm default cap 4; outline_agent.py:732 retrieval wrapped in asyncio.to_thread (default executor). Observed: 19/20 threads futex_wait, 0 progress 8.8min, SIGKILL (brief).
findings.proposed_speedup: Keep basket sharding but REQUIRED guards: (a) run the compose MAP on a DEDICATED module-owned ThreadPoolExecutor, never nested inside an asyncio.to_thread that shares the loop default executor; (b) bound basket_workers*section_concurrency <= a fixed global thread budget and keep side-judge cap >= that in-flight count but still <=~4-8/host (do NOT jump to 48, which also kills the 429 de-storm); (c) add acquire_judge_slot(timeout=...) on the compose path so a wedged holder force-frees its slot (JudgeSlotTimeout degrade already exists at judge_concurrency.py:165-176); (d) per-section watchdog wall so a stalled MAP cancels instead of SIGKILL.
findings.quality_risk: I4 (no-deadlock): as-attempted it hangs -> DISQUALIFIED until (a)-(d). I2 (verdict-identity): sharding is verdict-safe ONLY because the REDUCE stays serial in original basket order (verified_compose.py:2904-2953) — never parallelize the reduce; prove kept/dropped NLI set identical at full 328 scale.
findings.est_saving: Compose is the smaller slice per the brief; single-digit minutes at best, and only if the deadlock guards hold.

findings.what: TURN CAP (lower PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS=24 / PG_REACT_MAX_ITERATIONS to end the agentic retrieval loop sooner). This directly attacks the ~24min dominant phase but by reducing retrieval depth.
findings.evidence: outline_agent.py:106-108 (max_turns=24, wall=900s), :1885 loop guard, :1898 finish_outline BOUNCES while pending gaps + budget remain; react_agent.py:45 PG_REACT_MAX_ITERATIONS default 5.
findings.proposed_speedup: REJECT as a primary lever. Only legitimate if the loop is PROVABLY converged (gap_ledger pending_count==0 and no checklist deficiencies), in which case cutting turns changes nothing; otherwise it is speed-by-dropping-coverage.
findings.quality_risk: I1 (deep coverage): fewer turns = fewer baskets discovered/filled -> renders <328/329. I3 (agentic): a hard cap that leaves pending gaps is a quieter degrade of agentic depth. DISQUALIFIED.
findings.est_saving: Large (attacks the 24min pole) but comes entirely out of coverage — not a legal saving.

findings.what: BATCHED JUDGE (coalesce many NLI/entailment claims into one provider call to cut POST count on the verify path).
findings.evidence: strict_verify.py:1-40 defines checks per-sentence incl. entailment check (f); judge_concurrency.py:17-24 notes each side judge is one blocking POST per claim; NLI kept/dropped set is per-sentence.
findings.proposed_speedup: Only adopt if batching is a pure transport coalesce producing BYTE-IDENTICAL per-claim verdicts (same model/prompt/temp/max_tokens/JSON parsing, no shared cross-claim context, deterministic split). Must be proven verdict-identical at full scale before adoption.
findings.quality_risk: I2 (verdict-identity): batching N claims into one prompt changes model INPUT (cross-claim context, shared token budget, truncation) -> a per-claim verdict can flip vs one-POST-per-claim -> changes the kept/dropped set = weakened verify gate. DISQUALIFIED unless proven identical. (Mild I4 positive: fewer POSTs eases semaphore pressure.)
findings.est_saving: Moderate on the verify slice only; net negative if it forces a re-verification A/B each run.

findings.what: PARALLEL RETRIEVAL (fan out the per-turn fetch / multiple queries per turn). Retrieval is currently ONE query per turn wrapped in asyncio.to_thread(run_live_retrieval) — the serial long pole of the ~24min phase.
findings.evidence: outline_agent.py:732 (asyncio.to_thread, one query/turn), :740 per-round try/except returns failed ToolResult (loop continues), :752-757 order-sensitive fold-in (url-dedup->offset-renumber->S2 stamp); parallel_fetch.py substrate exists but is NOT wired to live_retriever (phase-1 only).
findings.proposed_speedup: Fan out fetches on a DEDICATED ThreadPoolExecutor (never the shared to_thread default pool), keep the per-fetch timeout (fetch_limiter FETCH_TIMEOUT=30), then fold all results in a DETERMINISTIC canonical order (sort by candidate/url key) before the serial fold-in — mirroring the compose MAP-then-serial-REDUCE pattern. Contain all fan-out exceptions in the existing per-round try/except.
findings.quality_risk: I4: N nested to_thread fetches can saturate the loop default executor -> use a dedicated pool. I2: fan-out changes row ARRIVAL ORDER and the fold-in dedup is order-sensitive -> canonicalize order before folding, then A/B the NLI kept set at full 328 scale. I3: keep fan-out exceptions inside :740 so they never escape to the :2061 degrade-to-seed path. SAFE IF GUARDED — does not drop baskets.
findings.est_saving: Potentially the biggest legal win (directly parallelizes the dominant serial fetch pole), on the order of several to ~10+ min if fetch first-byte latency dominates each turn.

findings.what: FETCH CACHE (reuse source-fetch responses; retrieval_cache.py) to avoid re-paying first-byte latency across turns/re-runs.
findings.evidence: retrieval_cache.py header: per-workspace SQLite cache with eviction API (evict_by_url, evict_older_than); module EXPLICITLY rejects pure TTL because a retracted/superseded source is a faithfulness liability.
findings.proposed_speedup: Cache the fetched BYTES only (keyed by canonicalized DOI/PMID/URL); keep strict_verify + NLI running LIVE on cached content so the verdict is recomputed fresh, never cached. Wire eviction-on-retraction.
findings.quality_risk: I2 (faithfulness): stale cached source can keep a claim whose evidence was retracted -> mitigated by eviction API (not pure TTL) and by recomputing the verdict live. Cache-key canonicalization must not merge two DIFFERENT sources. Coverage-neutral (I1) and cannot degrade (I3). SAFE IF GUARDED.
findings.est_saving: Run-dependent; largest on re-runs / overlapping queries. Zero on a cold single run.

findings.what: OFF-LOOP-ONLY COMPOSE (the already-ported verdict-safe to_thread wrap with PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS<=1). This is the safe floor.
findings.evidence: verified_compose.py:2851-2852 and :2997-2999 — workers<=1 skips the parallel branch and runs the ORIGINAL serial loop byte-identical.
findings.proposed_speedup: Keep it as the guaranteed-safe baseline (moves compose off the event loop so async retrieval overlaps), but run even this on a dedicated executor rather than the shared asyncio.to_thread default pool to avoid contention with retrieval.
findings.quality_risk: None of I1/I2/I3 by construction (byte-identical serial). Residual I4 only from sharing the default to_thread executor -> use a dedicated pool. SAFE.
findings.est_saving: Small — compose is the smaller slice; win is only the overlap with retrieval.

findings.what: LOWER VERIFY CONCURRENCY (reduce PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY or verifier semaphores) proposed as a stability/speed knob.
findings.evidence: judge_concurrency.py:17-24 — the side-judge cap is a faithfulness-STRENGTHENING de-storm: fewer simultaneous POSTs -> fewer 429 -> fewer retry-exhaustion fail-CLOSED drops -> MORE claims verified with the SAME verdicts; :31-37 default 4, validated band ~4-8/host.
findings.proposed_speedup: Treat it as a correctness knob, not a speed lever. Keep it in the validated ~4-8/host band. Flag any plan that lowers it for stability (slows verify) or raises it toward 48 for speed (re-introduces the 429 storm).
findings.quality_risk: I2: raising it too far re-introduces 429 fail-CLOSED drops that CHANGE the NLI kept set; lowering it only slows verify. I3: a 429 storm can push the outline past wall(900s) -> degrade-to-seed. NOT A SAFE SPEEDUP (wrong direction).
findings.est_saving: None (either slows verify or risks verdict-changing drops).

findings.what: Phase-by-phase wall-time split of the one CLEAN completed route_all=329-basket deep run (logs/step4_route_all_compose.log, elapsed_seconds=1449.7 / 24.2 min). Phases by timestamp: A Init+outline-seed-digest 04:10:43->04:13:05; B agentic retrieval react loop 04:13:05->04:25:22; C outline finalize+content_dedup+consolidate+credibility 04:25:22->04:25:55; D compose+strict_verify+repair+regen 04:25:55->04:34:29; E fact_dedup+analyst+render 04:34:29->04:34:56.
findings.evidence: Measured from step4_route_all_compose.log. Durations: B=737s (50.8%), D=514s (35.5%), A=142s (9.8%), C=33s (2.3%), E=27s (1.9%). Sum=1453s ~= reported elapsed 1449.7s. Credibility pass is free ('no production credibility judge wired (judge=None)', priors-only). RACE score is NOT in any render log (separate downstream eval harness) so it cannot be measured here.
findings.proposed_speedup: Rank by time: 1) retrieval 737s, 2) compose+verify 514s, 3) outline seed digest 142s, 4) dedup/consolidate/cred 33s, 5) fact_dedup/render 27s. AFTER retrieval the single biggest lever is COMPOSE+strict_verify (514s / 35.5%). dedup/consolidate/credibility/RACE are negligible or absent and not worth optimizing.
findings.quality_risk: None from measuring. The compose lever touches I2 (faithfulness) and I4 (deadlock) — see next findings.
findings.est_saving: identifies where the ~9 min of post-retrieval wall-time lives

findings.what: COMPOSE is the biggest post-retrieval lever (Phase D, 514s). It runs PER-SECTION (8 sections) under a concurrency semaphore of max=5 in BOTH clean step3 and step4 runs (the 48-way was the deadlock run). strict_verify is a LOCAL CrossEncoder NLI (consolidation_nli.entails_directional, nli-deberta-v3-base on cuda:0) with ZERO OpenRouter spend — so the verify gate is cheap; the cost is the LLM generation around it.
findings.evidence: step4/step3 logs: 'Concurrency semaphore (re)initialized: max=5' (38 occurrences). multi_section_generator.py drives per-section draft->strict_verify->repair_loop->regen. consolidation_nli.py: CrossEncoder(model_id) on cuda:0, 'ZERO new model / OpenRouter spend'.
findings.proposed_speedup: Raise section-level compose concurrency from max=5 toward a tested sweet spot (8-16, the compose_fix 16-way) so per-section repair/regen latency overlaps. This shrinks the 514s compose phase.
findings.quality_risk: I4 (deadlock): the 48-way DEADLOCKED (19/20 threads futex_wait, SIGKILL). Must stay below that ceiling, use a dedicated BOUNDED thread pool (not unbounded semaphore(48)), keep the OFF-LOOP to_thread wrap. I2: verdict-identical because each section's strict_verify runs unchanged; must A/B at full 328 scale.
findings.est_saving: up to ~2-4 min off the 514s compose phase if concurrency 5->~12 without deadlock

findings.what: The 41% sentence-drop rate DOES waste compute — but the waste is in LLM generation + repair_loop + full-section regen, NOT in verification (NLI is local/cheap). Drop rates measured: step3_control 64/155=41.3% (the '41%'), step3_treatment 70/150=46.7%, step4 68/133=51.1%.
findings.evidence: step4 Phase D = 38 compose LLM generate calls for 8 sections = 10 big draft/regen + 28 small (<3k in_tok) repair_loop calls (repair attempts up to =10 for one section). 2 of 8 sections REGENERATED ('post-M-41c kept_fraction=0.36 below min 0.40 — retrying'; Empirical=0.32). Worst case step3_treatment 'Automation, Tasks...' section: verified=0 dropped=16 regen=True — drafted TWICE, produced ZERO surviving sentences.
findings.proposed_speedup: Reduce drop-driven waste WITHOUT touching the gate: (a) raise first-pass entailment yield via tighter per-sentence span grounding in the draft prompt so fewer drafts fail; (b) skip the whole-section regen when it historically converges to verified~=0 (a redraft that yields 0 verified sentences is pure cost). Estimated ~130-180s of the 514s compose phase is drop-driven repair+regen.
findings.quality_risk: I2 (faithfulness): must change only what is FED to strict_verify, never the gate — kept/dropped NLI verdict set stays byte-identical. Do NOT lower the drop rate by loosening verification. Skipping a regen must never drop a section below coverage (I1) or degrade to seed (I3).
findings.est_saving: ~1-2 min off compose by cutting non-converging regens + repair round-trips

findings.what: Turn-level serialization of the react loop: gaps are drained ONE per turn with a full per-section after_fold checklist LLM re-run between every fetch (outline_agent.py:1885 run loop, :1252 _run_checklist, :1620 _execute).
findings.evidence: Measured in logs/step4_route_all_compose.log: retrieval/outline phase = 876s (14.6 min, 60% of the 1449.7s deep render). React loop 04:13:05->04:25:22 = 737s; 13 search_more_evidence calls totalled 346.3s of fetch, so ~391s (53% of the loop) is NON-fetch LLM overhead = decide + 11 after_fold checklists + fold-in topic-judge. Seed checklist named 3 gaps at once (04:13:05) yet they were drained one-per-turn. Corroborated: seed-checklist->outline-final was 898s/922s/1062s in step2/step3_treatment/_16way logs.
findings.proposed_speedup: R1: fetch the N PENDING ledger gaps concurrently (bounded 3-4) on a DEDICATED ThreadPoolExecutor, fold results in SEQUENTIALLY in a deterministic sorted order, and run the after_fold checklist ONCE per batch instead of once per fetch. Collapses ~13 sequential turns into ~4-5 rounds and drops the redundant between-fetch checklists.
findings.quality_risk: I2 FAITHFULNESS: fold_in_fetched_rows (url-dedup->offset-renumber hard assert->S2 stamp) is order-sensitive, so keep fold-in SERIAL in canonical url-key order — parallelize only fetches; search_more_evidence only ADDS candidate rows, strict_verify+NLI kept/dropped set stays downstream-identical. I4 DEADLOCK: MUST use a module-owned executor, NOT asyncio.to_thread's shared loop-default pool (that shared-executor nesting is what deadlocked at 328 scale); cap concurrency low since the outline agent runs ONCE per report. I3: contain per-round exceptions (existing :740 try/except) so nothing trips degrade-to-seed at :2054. Full-328 A/B verdict-identity + assert cp4_used=agentic required.

findings.what: Zero-yield redundant re-fetches: the same aspect is re-worded and re-searched repeatedly, each fetch returning only URLs already in ev_store (kept=0). No query->URL-set cache and no per-aspect URL-exhaustion breaker; the only veto (outline_agent.py:1630) covers unhomeable SECTIONS, not exhausted aspects.
findings.evidence: Four consecutive searches at 04:20:01/04:20:36/04:21:08/04:21:59 for 'impacts across various industries' each fetched 8-10 URLs but kept=0 (url-dup dropped 9/9,10/10,8/8,9/9). The whole 04:19:35->04:22:34 stretch = 179s (24% of the react loop) produced ZERO new rows.
findings.proposed_speedup: R2: cache Serper query->URL-set within a run; when a derived query's candidate URL set is >=~90% already in ev_store, SKIP the fetch and route the aspect straight to UNFILLED+disclosed (module already discloses at the retry cap) instead of re-firing.
findings.quality_risk: I1 COVERAGE: does NOT drop any basket — baskets are the pre-existing 329 clusters; this only stops re-fetching evidence already pooled, and disclosure is preserved. GUARD: key the breaker on URL-SET overlap AFTER the same url-dedup rule, never on query TEXT, so a genuinely-new query is never suppressed. Verdicts unaffected (no new rows means no NLI-set change).

findings.what: Heavy _decide: reasoning_effort=high + max_tokens=131072 for a menu pick over ~5 tools (outline_agent.py:1218-1227); query_derive also defaults max_tokens=131072 (:134).
findings.evidence: One post-bounce _decide took ~62s (04:22:34->~04:23:36) in the step4 log; decide+query-derive contribute to the ~391s non-fetch overhead measured above; decide wall is 660s.
findings.proposed_speedup: R3: drop the _decide call only to reasoning_effort=medium / smaller reasoning_max_tokens, and lower PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS from 131072.
findings.quality_risk: decide is a routing choice, not a faithfulness judgment; checklist critic + strict_verify + NLI untouched. RISK I2: it CAN change which gaps get searched -> different evidence pool -> not strictly verdict-identical, so gate behind full-scale verdict-identity A/B; lower priority than R1/R2.

findings.what: Within-search URL fetching is already parallel — so intra-search fetch concurrency is NOT the bottleneck (rules out one hypothesis).
findings.evidence: live_retriever.py:6363-6441: parallel_fetch(max_workers=band 8-14 scaled by candidate count, per_task_timeout=120s, per-host cap 4). Measured per-search fetch 12.7-56.3s is bounded by the slowest URL in a ~8-23 URL batch, not sequential per-URL fetching.
findings.proposed_speedup: No change here; do NOT invest in intra-search fetch concurrency. Focus effort on R1/R2 (turn-level parallelism + exhaustion breaker), which target the real ~53% non-fetch overhead and ~24% zero-yield waste.
findings.quality_risk: N/A — this is a negative finding that redirects effort away from a dead end; raising max_workers further would add provider load with no measured benefit.

result.real_saving: ~0 in the only legal (already-converged) case; the loop already exits early via the ACCEPTED path before hitting the cap, so cutting max_turns saves no wall-time there. Any "large" saving against the 24-min agentic pole is realized only by exiting while pending gaps remain — i.e. purchased directly out of coverage, not a legal saving.
result.reason: DISQUALIFIED. The react loop is coverage-bound, not idle-bound. Confirmed statically in the outline_agent worktree: outline_agent.py:1885 gates the loop on `turn < max_turns AND elapsed < wall`; on finish_outline (:1894-1909) it reads gap_ledger.pending_count and BOUNCES (`continue`) whenever `(deficiencies or pending) and budget_remains`, only breaking when pending==0 with no new deficiencies OR when budget is already gone. Todos still PENDING at exit are recorded UNFILLED (:1917-1922). Therefore lowering max_turns/wall makes budget_remains go False earlier while pending>0, leaving baskets undiscovered/unfilled. This violates I1 (deep coverage -> renders <328/329) and I3 (agentic: a hard cap that exits with pending gaps is exactly the seed+partial degrade, not cp4_used=agentic to convergence). The only case where cutting turns is harmless is a provably converged loop (pending_count==0, no deficiencies) — but there the loop already breaks via the ACCEPTED path (:1905-1909) before reaching the cap, so the cap is not binding and the change saves nothing. There is no regime where this both saves wall-time AND preserves coverage. Findings written to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result.real_saving: None. PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY is a correctness/de-storm knob, not a speed lever. It gates only the three verify-path side judges (entailment_judge, credibility_judge_caller, semantic_conflict_detector) in the COMPOSE/verify phase — NOT the agentic-retrieval react loop that dominates wall time (~24 of ~30 min), so it cannot touch the dominant slice. Lowering it only slows verify (negative saving); raising it delivers no legitimate speed, only risk.
result.reason: Adversarial verdict: NOT a safe speedup, wrong direction. Code-verified in both worktrees (byte-identical diff). judge_concurrency.py:17-24,31-37,51-55 documents this as a faithfulness-STRENGTHENING de-storm: default 4, memory standard <=4 in flight, validated band ~4-8/host; fewer simultaneous mirror-chain POSTs -> fewer 429 -> fewer retry-exhaustion fail-CLOSED ('ENTAILED','judge_error') drops -> MORE claims verified with the SAME verdicts. Both directions fail: (1) RAISE toward 48 re-introduces the 429 storm the cap cures -> fail-CLOSED drops CHANGE the NLI kept set (violates INVARIANT 2, faithfulness verdict-identity), and the 15-50s rate-limit backoffs can push the outline past PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_WALL_SECONDS_DEFAULT=900 (outline_agent.py:108) -> degrade-to-seed (violates INVARIANT 3, cp4_used=agentic). (2) LOWER below band only slows the verify pass = negative saving, no benefit. Default-FALSE applies on both grounds (marginal/negative saving AND invariant risk). Confirmed callers at entailment_judge.py:155, credibility_judge_caller.py:143, semantic_conflict_detector.py:125 are all verify-path, not the retrieval loop. Findings appended to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result.real_saving: ~0 runtime wall-time. This is a negative/anti-finding, not a runtime speedup: it correctly recommends NOT building intra-search fetch concurrency (which already exists) and redirecting effort to R1/R2. The only 'saving' is avoided wasted engineering effort plus avoided added provider load / futex-contention risk.
result.reason: Evidence verified against live code and the measured log. live_retriever.py:6363-6441 confirms intra-search URL fetch is ALREADY parallel: max_workers band 8-14 (constants _FETCH_WORKERS_FLOOR=8 / _CEILING=14 at lines 149/156, per_candidate=16), per-host cap 4, per_task_timeout=120s, host-keyed semaphore so distinct hosts fetch concurrently. Per-search wall is bounded by the slowest URL in the batch, not sequential fetching. The measured breakdown corroborates the cited waste: loop 737s, fetch 346s -> 391s non-fetch = 53%; zero-yield stretch 179s/737s = 24%. So fetch-layer concurrency is provably NOT the bottleneck; the real waste is turn serialization + redundant re-fetch, which R1/R2 target. keeps_quality=TRUE because the finding prescribes no code change: coverage, faithfulness verdict-identity, cp4_used=agentic, and no-deadlock are all trivially preserved, and it further AVOIDS the deadlock/provider-load risk that raising max_workers would introduce. The finding is sound; the only honest correction is that its 'saving' is effort-avoidance, not measurable wall-time.

result.real_saving: Marginal-to-zero real wall-time saving. The verify/entailment POSTs are a sub-slice of the COMPOSE phase, which is itself the SMALLER slice of the 30+min render (dominated by the ~24min agentic retrieval). Those POSTs are already idempotent-cached per (sentence,span) and already bounded to concurrency 4 with burst-spread, so the transport-pressure win the batch claims (I4) is ALREADY banked faithfully by judge_concurrency.py's semaphore. If PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off (a real production mode) there is no per-claim POST to batch and the saving is exactly zero. And because verdict-identity cannot be assumed, adoption forces a full-scale re-verification A/B EVERY run = the claimed net-negative is real.
result.reason: DISQUALIFIED on I2 (verdict-identity), and the saving is marginal even if it worked. Evidence (outline_agent, read-only): strict_verify.py:529 calls _get_judge().judge(sentence_clean, combined_span) ONCE PER SENTENCE, default mode "enforce" (I-bug-095); entailment_judge.py:792 formats a template that is a SINGLE (span,sentence) pair; :830-835 posts one user message at temperature=0.0, max_tokens=131072 (chain-min), reasoning.effort=high, on GLM-5.1, a REASONING model. Batching N claims into one prompt is a MODEL-INPUT change, not a transport coalesce, and violates every clause of the speedup's own admission gate: (1) "same prompt" fails — a batch needs a new N-pair template; (2) "no cross-claim context" fails — N claims share one context window so claim k's verdict can shift given claims 1..k-1; (3) "same max_tokens" fails in effect — the 131072 budget becomes SHARED across N reasoning traces, and later claims truncate mid-reasoning -> finish=length -> empty content -> ('ENTAILED','judge_error:...') sentinel -> fail-closed DROP in enforce (the exact arch-002/arch-004-F19 collapse, re-introduced N-fold), flipping the kept/dropped NLI set (I2) and risking mass drops (I1 coverage); (4) "same JSON parsing" fails — _extract_first_json_object takes the EARLIEST top-level JSON value (one verdict), so a batch needs a new N-element parser; (5) "deterministic split" is not guaranteed on a free-text reasoning model that can reorder/merge/omit list items, silently misaligning verdict[i]->claim[i]. It also destroys the per-(sentence,span) verdict cache key. Net: cannot be assumed verdict-identical, so it weakens the faithfulness gate; the only real win it offers (fewer POSTs) is already delivered faithfully by the existing bounded semaphore. Full findings appended to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result.real_saving: 0 s of wall-time from the finding itself — it is a profiling attribution, not a code change. The phase split verifies against the log: A seed-digest 143s, B retrieval 737s, C dedup/cred 33s, D compose+strict_verify 513s, E fact_dedup/render 27s; sum=1453s ≈ elapsed 1449.7s (baskets=329). Compose+verify is genuinely the #2 post-retrieval slice at 514s/35.5% OF THIS RUN, but that only tells you where to optimize; it saves nothing on its own.
result.reason: The attribution is accurate and reproducible from step4_route_all_compose.log — I reconstructed every phase boundary from real markers (last ReactDecision 04:24:44 -> content_dedup 04:25:55 -> fact_dedup 04:34:29 -> elapsed 1449.7s) and the five durations sum to the reported elapsed. All four invariants held on this run (baskets=329, kept=8/dropped_sections=0 coverage; faithfulness_pass=true, leaked_cite_ev_tokens=0, unresolved_markers=[] faithfulness; finish_outline BOUNCED and kept looping = agentic, no degrade-to-seed; clean completion, no SIGKILL). Since measuring changes nothing, it cannot threaten any invariant. But keeps_quality=FALSE because this is a DIAGNOSIS, not a speedup: real wall-time saved = 0s. Two adversarial caveats: (1) the 35.5% compose share is measured on a 24.2min/12.3min-retrieval run, whereas the brief's established deep render is 30+min with ~24min retrieval — at true full scale retrieval's fraction grows and compose's shrinks, so the specific percentage is NOT portable (only the ranking retrieval>compose is robust); (2) the actual optimization implied by 'the compose lever' is basket-worker sharding, which is the exact path that already DEADLOCKED (19/20 threads futex_wait, SIGKILL) and touches I2/I4 — so the lever this finding points at is precisely the risky one, not a free win. Keep the numbers as a routing aid; do not score them as a wall-time saving.

result.real_saving: ~0 on the target workload (a single cold route_all 328-basket deep render) — the proposer admits "zero on a cold single run," and that single render IS the measured ~30-min problem. Nonzero only on re-runs/overlapping queries (a different workload), and even there it shaves only HTTP-fetch I/O, not the dominant cost. Not measurable as-is because the cache is not wired to the hot path (dead code on the render path).
result.reason: Code-verified in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (findings written to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md). (1) NOT WIRED: `grep -rn 'RetrievalCacheStore(' src` finds zero production instantiations — it's constructed only in tests; the real hot fetch path live_retriever.run_live_retrieval (httpx) never references retrieval_cache/make_cache_key; cache_warming.py and freshness_monitor.py are not called by any orchestration/graph entrypoint. So as shipped it saves 0, and realizing any saving needs edits to live_retriever's fetch loop — forbidden (READ-ONLY, live wheel). (2) WRONG SLICE: the dominant ~24/30 min is the agentic react loop, serialized one-turn-at-a-time because each turn's _decide (react_agent.py:715) is an LLM call choosing the next URL from prior results; a bytes cache only shortens the fetch inside _execute_tool, never the serial LLM-decision latency that makes turns serial. (3) ZERO ON STATED PROBLEM by proposer's own admission (cold single render). (4) Intra-run overlap already partly covered by live_retriever's versioned authority_enrich.sqlite cache and process-local negative refetch cache. (5) Residual I2 faithfulness risk: verdict recomputed live on cached BYTES is only as faithful as the bytes; a source retracted between runs with the eviction daemon not firing lets live NLI KEEP a claim whose evidence was retracted — eviction is event-driven, not guaranteed, and there is no content-version force-rebuild guard like authority_enrich has. Default-FALSE applies: saving is zero/marginal on the actual workload AND it carries a residual faithfulness risk.

result.real_saving: ~0 (unmeasurable). The claimed "overlap with retrieval" cannot occur: graph_v2.py:641-675 makes the pipeline strictly sequential (plan_outline = the ~24min react retrieval loop, run_live_retrieval via to_thread at outline_agent.py:732) -> blueprint -> write_one_section (compose). Retrieval fully completes before compose starts; they are separate graph nodes on a hard edge and never run concurrently. The off-loop to_thread(compose) wrap is ALREADY the shipped baseline (multi_section_generator.py:6079,:6130); the only delta proposed is default-pool -> dedicated-pool, which removes contention-with-retrieval that does not exist during the compose phase and is unmeasurable over the existing baseline.
result.reason: The change preserves all 4 invariants (I1 coverage, I2 faithfulness verdict-identity, I3 agentic, I4 no-deadlock) because at PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=1 the parallel branch is skipped (verified_compose.py:2851-2854/2955/2997-2999), so compose runs the ORIGINAL serial loop byte-identical with NO nested ThreadPoolExecutor - the futex_wait storm (a workers>1 + sem(48) phenomenon) structurally cannot occur, and a dedicated pool adds no reentrancy. BUT keeps_quality is FALSE under the task rule (default FALSE if the saving is marginal / it must REALLY save meaningful wall-time): the saving is not merely small, it is nonexistent. The stated mechanism ('moves compose off the event loop so async retrieval overlaps' / 'dedicated executor to avoid contention with retrieval') is factually wrong for this pipeline - retrieval and compose are sequential phases with zero temporal overlap. The genuine (small) value of off-loading compose is intra-compose section parallelism (fan_out_write / PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS keeping sibling async writer/verify HTTP unfrozen), and that is already delivered by the shipped baseline, not by the dedicated-executor delta being verified. So this specific proposal is a safe no-op, not a speedup. Findings written to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result.real_saving: Marginal and overstated. Measured from the one clean 329-basket deep run (logs/step4_route_all_compose.log): the ENTIRE fetch pole across all 13 react turns is 346.3s = 5.77 min (per-fetch 12.7-56.3s, max 56.3s), so a "several to ~10+ min" saving is physically impossible. Intra-search fetch is ALREADY parallel (live_retriever.py:6437 parallel_fetch, max_workers 8-14, per-host cap 4, own pool), so the only serial pole is cross-turn, and cross-turn fetch is entangled with the serial _decide loop (391s = 53% of the 737s loop) that this speedup does NOT touch. Absolute ceiling if all 13 fetches perfectly overlapped is ~4.8 min, and even that requires ALSO collapsing decide/checklist. Real achievable saving is a fraction of that.
result.reason: FALSE on two independent grounds. (1) Saving is marginal/overstated: the fetch pole is only 5.77 min total and is already intra-search parallel; the true dominant serial pole is the decide+checklist LLM overhead (391s/53%), which the stated speedup leaves untouched. (2) I2 verdict-identity is concretely at risk and the named guard is INSUFFICIENT: _fold_in.py:167-172 dedups fetched rows against workspace.existing_urls() = the store as mutated by PRIOR turns. The serial loop drops cross-turn url-dups only because each turn inserts before the next dedups (measured W2: url-dup dropped 9/9,10/10,8/8,9/9 = 36 rows). Batching/re-sorting N turns' rows into one fold breaks that: intra-batch same-url rows both survive :168-172, get distinct ids, and flow into the S2/off-topic screen, so extra rows can survive -> different evidence pool -> the NLI kept/dropped set can change. "Canonical sorted order" is deterministic but NOT identical to the serial baseline; the only I2-safe fold is turn-by-turn in exact baseline gap order with identical incremental dedup, which is not what "sort by url key" does, and it is unproven at 328 scale. I3 is a secondary risk (fan-out requires draining gaps without re-running _decide, a control-flow change). Only I4 (dedicated small off-loop pool) and I1 (no basket drop) are satisfiable, which does not rescue the rest. Findings appended to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result.real_saving: Marginal and largely speculative. In the clean 329-basket run (logs/step4_route_all_compose.log, 1449.7s total) all 24 _decide calls summed to ~170s (11.7%), each already terminating at 142-1894 reasoning tokens — far below the 32768 ceiling. (a) effort high->medium might shave a few s/call = tens of seconds best case (~2-4% of wall, vs fetch=50.8%/compose=35.5%); (b) smaller reasoning_max_tokens saves ~0s on the happy path (ceiling never approached); (c) lowering PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS saves ~0s (derived query is one line, 5-140 bytes). The cited 62s decide is a bad-tail outlier medium-effort would not reliably remove.
result.reason: Fails I2 by construction (admitted by the proposal's own RISK I2): _decide's reasoning is the routing deliberation that selects which gap to search and, via query_derive, the query string. effort high->medium changes that deliberation -> different search_more_evidence queries -> different fetched URLs -> different evidence pool -> different NLI kept/dropped set. Routing selects the evidence faithfulness is computed over, so the gate stays intact but its INPUT changes = NOT verdict-identical. Sub-lever (b) is redundant on the happy path (reasoning already terminates <1894 tok vs 32768 ceiling) and dangerous on dense corpora: the code header (outline_agent.py:110-134) documents the ceiling was RAISED specifically because glm-5.2's prelude hit ~21k tokens and threw ReasoningFirstTruncationError; a smaller ceiling re-arms that crash, which in _decide is caught at :1888-1892 and BREAKS the loop early leaving pending gaps unfilled -> fewer baskets (I1 coverage loss), and in query_derive fail-opens to the raw aspect string (:699) -> different query -> I2 again. The knob is shared (PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS) so it cannot be lowered for decide alone. Sub-lever (c) has no upside (one-line output) and only a truncation downside. Saving is marginal (~2-4% at best) and two of three sub-levers save nothing. Gating behind a full-scale A/B does not rescue it — the A/B would simply measure the verdict drift and any non-identical kept/dropped set fails I2. Findings written to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result.real_saving: Marginal / not meaningful. Compose is the smaller slice; retrieval (~24 min of the ~30+ min end-to-end) is the long pole. Proposer's own claim is 'single-digit minutes at best,' and only if the deadlock guards hold. I could NOT run a full 328-basket render to measure (read-only mandate + 30+ min cost + real SIGKILL/deadlock risk), so I report the brief's and proposer's stated split rather than a fresh measured number. Even an ideal compose parallelization cannot dent the agentic long pole.
result.reason:
Structurally, the good part checks out: the REDUCE stays serial in original basket order (verified_compose.py:3525-3533, `list(_ex.map(_map_one_basket, ...))` then serial `_reduce_candidates`), and MAP reads no survivor state, so sharding does not threaten coverage (I1) or agentic (I3) and the dedup order is preserved. But it fails on I4 and I2, and the saving is marginal:

1) Saving is marginal — compose is the smaller slice vs the ~24 min retrieval phase; proposer concedes single-digit minutes. Wrong lane for wall-time.

2) I4 (no-deadlock) is UNRESOLVED. Guards (a)-(d) are a design proposal, not code. The real nesting is confirmed: `_compose_section_per_basket` runs via `await asyncio.to_thread(...)` (multi_section_generator.py:5476,5527) on the default executor, with parallel sections, then opens its own basket pool (verified_compose.py:3530), an inner synth-verify pool (:1851), and each verify hits entailment_judge -> acquire_judge_slot (shared sem, default 4) -> another ThreadPoolExecutor(1) (entailment_judge.py:155-158). That is 3-4 nested bounded pools plus one shared global semaphore — the starvation recipe that already SIGKILL-hung.

3) Guard (d) is not cleanly implementable: the codebase itself states `asyncio.to_thread is not cancellable` (multi_section_generator.py:9529,9563). A watchdog can only ABANDON a wedged MAP worker, and the basket pool's `with ThreadPoolExecutor(...) as _ex:` __exit__ joins (shutdown wait=True) on that wedged thread — re-hanging the section. Abandoned workers keep holding the shared judge sem and default-executor slots.

4) Guard (c) TENSIONS I2. The binding compose/entailment path acquires with NO timeout (entailment_judge.py:155 `with _acquire_judge_slot():`); the JudgeSlotTimeout degrade is wired only for the ADVISORY credibility judge (a non-binding gate). Adding a timeout to the binding gate means a wedged-but-would-verify claim times out and its sentence is dropped/degraded — changing the kept/dropped NLI set. Force-freeing a slot on a binding verdict path is not verdict-neutral.

5) I2 is UNPROVEN at scale — the passing A/B never exercised full-scale concurrency, and the map now realizes cross-basket in-flight judge concurrency the serial loop never reached; no full-328 kept/dropped-NLI A/B exists.

The two guards that actually stop the hang are the two that are unimplemented and internally conflicted: (c) trades I2 for I4, and (d) cannot cancel a non-cancellable to_thread worker. Default to FALSE. Findings appended to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result.real_saving: Unverified as a whole-run number. The "~2-3 min" equals the single observed 179s pathological stretch (one aspect re-fired 4x at 100% URL overlap), not a measured whole-run aggregate; I could not run the full 328-basket render. R2 also still pays the Serper search + per-call derive-query LLM every time, saving only the fetch/classify slice. A verdict-safe 100%-overlap-only variant would reclaim strictly LESS than R2's 90% version.
result.reason: R2 fails on its load-bearing detail: the ~90% threshold. Verified against code (outline/_fold_in.py:167-173 url-dedup is a pure source_url-string match vs ev_store; run_live_retrieval live_retriever.py:5405 has no held-url param so it re-fetches then fold_in url-dups). At >=90% overlap, up to ~10% of candidate URLs are GENUINELY NEW; skipping the whole fetch discards them. Any such new URL whose row would survive fold-in (not url-dup/chrome/off-topic) would have entered ev_store and been eligible for strict_verify/NLI, so suppressing it CHANGES the evidence pool => can change the kept/dropped NLI verdict set (VIOLATES I2 verdict-identity) and can starve a basket of its only row (VIOLATES I1 328/329 coverage). R2's own claim "no new rows => no NLI-set change" is true ONLY at 100% overlap; the cited evidence (url-dup 9/9,10/10,8/8,9/9 => kept=0) is exactly the 100% case and does not support the 90% generalization. I4 no-deadlock is not threatened (R2 removes work). I3: routing to UNFILLED is the normal disclosed state and does not directly flip cp4_used, but aggressively forcing fillable aspects to UNFILLED nudges toward the "seed+partial under load" degradation. Only a strict 100%-overlap breaker, or a per-URL fetch-skip (which requires editing the forbidden live wheel), would be verdict-safe -- and neither is R2 as written. Default-to-false applies. Findings written to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result.real_saving: Unverified. The 130-180s/514s figure is the proposer's estimate and is not independently measurable here (full 328-basket compose is expensive; OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS records root-owned run_s5_i3.py jobs distorting compose timing). Restricted to a provably invariant-safe no-op, the real saving is ~0: the only structurally-safe way to cut regen cost is to make sections pass on the first draft, which is exactly part (a) — and part (a) is not verdict-identical.
result.reason: Fails on two independent counts. PART (a) tighter draft prompt VIOLATES I2 (faithfulness verdict-identity): changing the draft prompt changes the draft text -> different sentences are fed to strict_verify -> a different NLI kept/dropped verdict set. That is by definition NOT verdict-identical; the proposer's own reframe ('change only what is FED to the gate') concedes it changes the gate's input, which changes verdicts. It is also an unproven quality experiment (a stricter contract can lower yield/coverage, I1) that removes no LLM call by itself. PART (b) skip-non-converging-regen cannot be done invariant-safely: there is NO historical-convergence oracle in the pipeline (retrieval is live/agentic, evidence pools differ every run), so the only signal at decision time is the first-pass result. But first-pass verified=0 (the worst-case 'Automation, Tasks' section) is EXACTLY the coverage-RESCUE case — code-verified at multi_section_generator.py:5779-5786 (regen fires on post_filter_fraction<0.40) and :5833 (regen adopted iff it produces STRICTLY MORE kept than first pass), so with 0 first-pass kept any regen with >=1 kept rescues the section. Skipping regen there DROPS sections the regen would have rescued -> renders <328/329 (violates I1) and pressures the gap-stub/degrade path (I3). The regen is a genuinely different, stochastic sample (tighter_retry HARD CONTRACT at :3614-3654, cold temp 0.3->0.1 at :3693-3699), not a deterministic replay, so 'it converged to 0 once' does not prove it always will. The code comment at :3688-3692 shows the team already empirically tuned regen with cold temp precisely so it stops being zero-yield waste — the proposal would undo an earned fix. Default-FALSE applies: risks I1/I2/I3 and the saving is unverified/marginal.

result.real_saving: Unverified by measurement (no full ~24-min paid render run under the read-only/no-spend constraint). Structurally, the verdict-safe variant yields only a MARGINAL saving of roughly tens of seconds (~30-90s) on the ~514s compose phase, NOT the claimed 2-4 min. The claimed 2-4 min is structurally implausible without also lifting the global LLM cap.
result.reason:
KNOB MISIDENTIFICATION is the core defect. There are TWO nested compose caps: (A) the real section-level concurrency = PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS, an asyncio.Semaphore defaulting to 3 (multi_section_generator.py:10545-10556, fn default :9555); and (B) the GLOBAL LLM cap PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM defaulting to 5 (llm_provider.py:79,124-157), acquired by EVERY OpenRouter call (openrouter_client.py:1889-1891). The proposal's evidence — 'Concurrency semaphore (re)initialized: max=5' (38x) — is knob (B), the global cap, and those log lines are from the AGENTIC OUTLINE phase, NOT compose: in logs/step4_route_all_compose.log all 19 re-inits (04:10-04:25) interleave with update_outline/auto-assign/topic_gate (fresh-loop-per-turn react loop); re-inits stop before the section-write phase. So section concurrency is actually at 3, and '5' is the global cross-phase cap.

SAFE VARIANT (raise only PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS 3->8/12, leave global=5): keeps all 4 invariants — I2 verdict-identical by construction (code 10534-10544: sections generated/verified independently and IDENTICALLY, merged in original order; strict_verify unchanged), I4 no thread-deadlock (asyncio single-loop semaphore, NOT the PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS thread pool that futex-deadlocked at 48), I1/I3 untouched (compose-only). BUT section drafts still bottleneck on global sem=5, and NLI verify is separately capped at PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=4; with 8 sections and 133 NLI checks + regens (65 verified/68 dropped), the saving is marginal — far below 2-4 min.

CLAIMED-SAVING VARIANT: reaching 2-4 min requires ALSO lifting the global sem (B), which cannot be scoped to compose — it governs the dominant ~14-min agentic phase. That threatens I3 (brief-documented degrade-to-seed under load -> cp4_used != agentic via 429/timeout) and I1 (429 -> _TRANSIENT_SECTION_FAILURES -> gap-stub/dropped sections). Per default-to-FALSE: the framing targets the wrong '5', the safe knob's saving is marginal, and the aggressive knob risks I3/I1. Findings appended to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result.real_saving: Marginal / largely illusory. Of the 737s react loop (measured 04:13:05->04:25:22 in logs/step4_route_all_compose.log), 346.3s is fetch that run_live_retrieval ALREADY parallelizes internally over its own ThreadPoolExecutor (per-call 12-56s is the parallel latency of a ~10-URL batch), so a 4-wide outer fan-out adds little throughput; the other ~390s is decide+checklist LLM calls that R1 can only bank by dropping the after_fold checklist — which changes convergence. Net defensible wall saving is well under the claimed 3-5 min.
result.reason: Fails I2 verdict-identity, and the saving premise is inflated. TRACE EVIDENCE (route_all deep-render log): the 13 turns are NOT 13 independent, pre-batchable gaps — they are a PATH-DEPENDENT convergence loop. The after_fold checklist (:1862) is not a redundant between-fetch step: it is both the convergence gate (mark_complete :1873 vs mark_retry :1867) and gap-DETECTOR #1 that MINTS new gaps mid-loop (the aspect 'explicitly defining inclusion criteria...' is first minted at turn ~6, 04:18:10 — uncomputable in any seed-time batch). 1-2 sticky aspects re-fire in nearly every checklist, and turns ~8-11 all return kept=0 (url-dup churn) precisely because the serial checklist keeps re-naming them against the incrementally grown pool. Since the SET of queries fired is produced by the serial checklist sequence, batching + one-checklist-per-round fires a DIFFERENT query set -> folds a DIFFERENT URL set -> DIFFERENT ev_store -> DIFFERENT strict_verify+NLI kept/dropped set. The proposal's claim that 'search_more_evidence only ADDS rows, downstream identical' is refuted: the rows added are chosen by the very loop it alters, and the mandated full-328 A/B was NOT run. I4 also unretired: search_more_evidence nests to_thread(run_live_retrieval) at :732 and _fold_in to_thread(classify_topic_relevance) at :116 on the SHARED loop-default pool, and run_live_retrieval opens its own inner pools — running N concurrently multiplies the exact nested-shared-executor pattern that SIGKILL-deadlocked at 328 scale (lower risk here since outline runs once/report at N=3-4, but unproven at scale). It also violates the documented invariant at outline_agent.py:659-660 ('One retrieval in flight at a time... NEVER fired concurrently by the driver'). Default-to-FALSE is warranted: concrete I2 break + unproven I4 + marginal real saving. Findings appended to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

findings.what: Root cause of the 328-basket freeze: unclamped, multiplicative thread x shared-threading-semaphore OVER-SUBSCRIPTION with no timeout/no-wall backstop. NOT re-entrant, NOT cross-async-boundary.
findings.evidence: _compose_basket_workers() returns PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS verbatim with NO global clamp (verified_compose.py:232-243) and is applied PER SECTION, so live threads = PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS (multi_section_generator.py:10545-10556, default 3) x basket_workers, each POST also spawning an inner ThreadPoolExecutor(1) (entailment_judge.py:156) = ~2x amplification. All contend for ONE process-global threading.BoundedSemaphore acquire_judge_slot (judge_concurrency.py:137-182; run set it to 48). The binding entailment acquire has NO timeout: 'with _acquire_judge_slot():' (entailment_judge.py:155). The basket-map join list(TPE(_basket_workers).map(...)) (verified_compose.py:2992) and the outer asyncio.to_thread wrap (multi_section_generator.py:6079/6130) have no wall. Measured os.cpu_count()=128 => default asyncio.to_thread executor = min(32,132)=32 threads.
findings.proposed_speedup: Ship the OFF-LOOP-ONLY subset: asyncio.to_thread(_compose_section_per_basket) with PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=1. Gives real section-level parallelism (frees the event loop; sibling sections + writer callbacks + judge 429 backoffs stop freezing on the main loop) with exactly ONE judge-slot consumer per section-thread and no second unclamped tier. This is the piece the lane brief already certified verdict-safe.
findings.quality_risk: Threatens INVARIANT 4 (no deadlock) if basket_workers>1 without a clamp. Kept safe by (a) default basket_workers=1 (off-loop-only), and (b) the REDUCE stays serial+order-preserving so kept/dropped dedup is byte-identical (invariant 2 intact). Do NOT add a fail-closed timeout to the entailment acquire: JudgeSlotTimeout would emit the drop-sentinel and change the kept/dropped verdict set (violates invariant 2).
findings.est_saving: Recovers the compose phase from unrecoverable hang (SIGKILL) to completing; compose is the smaller slice vs the ~24min agentic phase, so real section-parallel compose saves on the order of the compose serial tail (minutes), not the dominant retrieval time.

findings.what: The exact deadlock-proof fix for basket_workers>1: clamp demand to judge-slot supply in _compose_basket_workers().
findings.evidence: _compose_basket_workers() (verified_compose.py:232-243) has no min() against any global; compose_fix's own comment (verified_compose.py:1816) merely CLAIMS the global judge semaphore bounds it, but that bounds POSTs, not the thread count that parks on the no-timeout acquire.
findings.proposed_speedup: In _compose_basket_workers(): judge_cap=resolve_max_concurrency() (0=>unbounded, no clamp); sections=max(1, PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS or max_parallel_sections); return max(1, min(raw, judge_cap // sections)). Guarantees sections x basket_workers <= judge_cap so no thread ever parks on an exhausted-forever semaphore; also set PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY >= sections x basket_workers for the run.
findings.quality_risk: Verdict-identical: the clamp changes only HOW MANY baskets compose at once; it never drops a claim and the serial order-preserving REDUCE keeps dedup byte-identical (invariants 1,2,3 intact). Keeps invariant 4 by construction (demand<=supply).
findings.est_saving: Enables safe intra-section basket concurrency on top of section concurrency without hang risk; incremental over subset A.

findings.what: Ruled-out hypotheses (so the fix does not chase the wrong lock).
findings.evidence: (1) NOT re-entrant: outline_agent _verify_all_sentences_synth is a plain SERIAL for-loop (verified_compose.py:1585-1611) - the PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY_SYNTH nested pool was NOT ported; verify holds <=1 slot at a time; the credibility pass (the other slot consumer) runs at 10190, BEFORE Stage-2 compose (10534), so no overlap. (2) NOT cross-async-boundary: async get_semaphore (llm_provider.py:124, asyncio.Semaphore) is used only by async OpenRouterClient._call; the off-loop compose uses the SYNC entailment judge (client.post on a worker thread, no run_coroutine_threadsafe). (3) NOT a shared-client close race: each worker gets its own threading.local() httpx client (entailment_judge.py:672-710).
findings.proposed_speedup: No code change; documents why compose_fix gated parallel-verify OFF by default = a CERTIFIABILITY guard (record/replay A/B cannot byte-certify the run-to-run-nondeterministic multi-basket path, verified_compose.py:1818-1826), NOT a deadlock guard - which is why the small A/B passed and the deadlock stayed latent.
findings.quality_risk: None (analysis only). Prevents mis-fixing that could relax faithfulness (invariant 2).

result.real_saving: ~0 wall-time. Analysis-only, no code change. It does not render any of the 328 baskets faster, and it does not even unlock a speedup: the synth-verify pool is not ported to outline_agent (PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY_SYNTH appears 0 times) and verify is a sub-slice of COMPOSE, while the profile shows AGENTIC RETRIEVAL (~24 min) dominates the ~30-min end-to-end. Cannot move the pole.
result.reason: All four evidence claims verified verbatim against the code: (1) compose_fix verified_compose.py:1818-1826 states the OFF-default is because the record/replay A/B certifier cannot byte-certify the run-to-run-nondeterministic multi-basket path and is 'faithfulness-neutral by construction' — a CERTIFIABILITY guard, not a deadlock guard, confirmed; (2) outline_agent verified_compose.py:1585-1611 _verify_all_sentences_synth is a plain serial for-loop, PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY_SYNTH grep count = 0, so the nested pool was never ported (not re-entrant, verify holds <=1 slot); (3) no run_coroutine_threadsafe in the outline_agent files and get_semaphore is an asyncio.Semaphore used only by the async client, so the sync off-loop judge does not cross the async boundary; (4) entailment_judge.py:680-710 gives each worker its own threading.local httpx client, so no shared-close race. The note is technically accurate and even useful (it correctly warns against a mis-fix that would relax the faithfulness gate, invariant 2) and, changing nothing, it trivially keeps all four invariants. BUT the adversarial question is whether it REALLY saves meaningful wall-time — it does not. It is a diagnostic note, not a speedup: claimed saving undefined, real saving ~0. It also does not unlock a speedup, because the actual full-scale deadlock lives in a DIFFERENT mechanism (PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS at verified_compose.py:232/2846 + semaphore(48)), which this note neither touches nor fixes, and verify parallelism cannot move the ~24-min agentic-retrieval pole anyway. Marginal/zero saving => default to FALSE. Findings written to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result.real_saving: None guaranteed. The exact proposed expression max(1, min(raw, resolve_max_concurrency()//sections)) yields 1 (SERIAL, zero saving) in the two configs that matter: (a) unbounded judge cap PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=0 -> 0//sections=0 -> 1 (the proposal's "0=>unbounded, no clamp" is arithmetically false); (b) the shipped default de-storm cap J=4 with default sections=3 -> 4//3 = 1. The only way to get real concurrency (e.g. 16 workers) is J~=48, which is an UNSAFE config (see below). So at every safe knob setting the speedup saves nothing measurable.
result.reason:
Disqualified on multiple independent grounds, code-verified in /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (findings appended to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md):

1) FORMULA BROKEN. resolve_max_concurrency() returns 0 for the unbounded escape hatch (judge_concurrency.py:100-101). 0//sections=0, so min(raw,0)=0, max(1,0)=1 -> forces SERIAL exactly when the operator disabled the cap. And at the default cap J=4 / default sections=3, workers=4//3=1 -> serial. Zero saving at safe defaults.

2) VIOLATES I1/I2 (the escape from #1 is unsafe). Getting real basket concurrency requires raising PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY well above 4 (the proposal even says set it >= sections*basket_workers, e.g. 48). DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY=4 exists precisely because ~32 simultaneous judge POSTs 429-stormed the provider (judge_concurrency.py:51-55). A 429 storm makes the entailment judge fail CLOSED (judge_error sentinel) which DROPS verified sentences -> changes the kept/dropped NLI set (I2) and can reduce coverage (I1). The clamp trades hang-risk for verdict-drift-risk.

3) DEMAND MODEL INCOMPLETE. Real leaf judge demand is sections*basket_workers*V where V is the inner PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY_SYNTH/PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY pool (verified_compose.py:1851), and each basket issues many sequential verify_fn calls. The clamp bounds only sections*basket_workers<=J; "demand<=supply by construction" holds only if V=1, which the proposal never pins.

4) MISDIAGNOSES I4 (no-deadlock). acquire_judge_slot is never exhausted-forever: _post_with_total_deadline (entailment_judge.py:155-183) holds a slot for ONE POST under a hard 150s force-close, then releases. Steady-state semaphore queueing self-heals in <=150s; the observed 8.8-min zero-progress SIGKILL hang is circular-wait/thread-exhaustion, not semaphore backlog. Compose is dispatched via asyncio.to_thread on the loop DEFAULT executor (multi_section_generator.py:5476/5527) and the basket ThreadPoolExecutor nests under it -- the exact nesting flagged as the deadlock root cause. The proposal implements none of the real guards (dedicated executor off the shared default pool; acquire-timeout on the compose path; per-section watchdog), so I4 is not established.

5) VERDICT-IDENTITY UNCERTIFIABLE (I2). The module's own comment (verified_compose.py:1820-1826) states the multi-basket compose path is non-deterministic run-to-run even fully serial and the certifier cannot byte-certify it; concurrency-induced verify timeouts under load can flip a verdict to a fail-closed DROP a serial run would not hit. Verdict-identity is asserted, not proven.

Net: at safe knobs the clamp forces serial (no saving); the only concurrent configs require an unsafe judge cap that risks faithfulness/coverage and still does nothing about the actual thread-exhaustion hang. Default to keeps_quality=FALSE.

result.real_saving: ~0 incremental. This config is already live: the off-loop asyncio.to_thread(_compose_section_per_basket) wrap is present at multi_section_generator.py:6079/6130 (outline_agent src has 7 to_thread calls vs the POLARIS baseline's 4 = the compose off-loop wraps already merged), and PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS defaults to 1 (verified_compose.py:238; not forced in .env or any launch_*.sh). A real route_all run on exactly this config (logs/step4_route_all_compose.log) already completed end-to-end in elapsed=1449.7s with NO SIGKILL (kept=8, verified=65, dropped=68). The claimed 'recover from SIGKILL' saving is measured only against the BROKEN workers>1 + semaphore(48) config, which is not a shippable baseline. Retrieval (~24min react loop) and compose are sequential langgraph nodes that never overlap, and compose is the smaller slice, so even perfect compose parallelism cannot dent the long pole.
result.reason: All 4 invariants are KEPT (workers=1 => serial byte-identical MAP+REDUCE => verdict-identical I1/I2; entailment acquire keeps its no-timeout binding so no drop-sentinel; compose is a downstream graph node so cp4_used/degrade-to-seed is untouched I3; no nested basket pool and <=3 concurrent judge-slot holders vs cap 48 so the 19/20 futex_wait storm cannot recur I4). BUT this proposal is not a NEW speedup — it is the already-live default state, so there is no incremental wall-time to recover. Per the 'default FALSE if the saving is marginal' rule, a zero-delta re-ship fails the 'REALLY saves meaningful wall-time' half of the test. The off-loop + workers=1 baseline is worth keeping; labelling it as a shippable speedup is a no-op. Full findings appended to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.

result:
POLARIS SPEED-VS-QUALITY — FINAL SYNTHESIS (written to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md, "FINAL SYNTHESIS" section)

Context that shapes everything below: after adversarial verification, exactly ONE finding survived, and it is a negative one — intra-search URL fetching is already parallel (live_retriever.py:6363-6441, workers band 8-14, per-host cap 4), so fetch concurrency is a dead end. Every affirmative speedup was disqualified on invariant or marginal-saving grounds. The honest plan is "protect the floor, fix the tail, defer one small safe lever."

(1) END-TO-END BREAKDOWN + BIGGEST HONEST LEVER (measured, logs/step4_route_all_compose.log, 329 baskets, completed clean, 1449.7s = 24.2 min):
- Seed outline digest 142s (9.8%) | AGENTIC REACT LOOP 737s (50.8%) = fetch 346.3s + non-fetch LLM ~391s (24 decides ≈ 170s + ~11 checklists); 179s (24%) of the loop was zero-yield re-fetch churn | dedup/consolidate 33s (2.3%) | COMPOSE + strict_verify 514s (35.5%) | render 27s (1.9%).
- The brief's "30+ min / ~24-min retrieval" is the BAD-TAIL case (_16way_run: one 466s mega-fetch of 162/200 URLs, then the 900s wall tripped → degrade-to-seed).
- Single biggest honest lever: TAIL CONTROL — the ~6-10 min gap between the clean 24.2-min run and the 30+-min bad-tail run. It is also an I3 quality bug (the tail run degraded to seed). No legal mechanism dents the clean-run 737s loop.

(2) RANKED PLAN (all 4 invariants kept, guard per item):
- P0 — Pin the safe compose config: shipped off-loop asyncio.to_thread wrap (multi_section_generator.py:6079/:6130) + PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=1 (confirmed current default; no .env/launch override) + judge cap in 4-8 band + PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3. GUARD: startup assert refusing workers>1 or sem≥48 until a re-engineered path passes a full-328 verdict-identity A/B. Saving vs live baseline: 0; vs the 16-way attempt: hang → completion.
- P1 — Fix the bad tail as a correctness bug (~6-10 min on affected runs + restores I3). No cleared mechanism yet; constraint: no fetch-cap/turn cuts; raising the outline wall is quality-preserving even though slower.
- P2 — 100%-overlap per-aspect exhaustion skip (only salvageable affirmative lever; DEFERRED to next wheel — needs the read-only live_retriever seam). Skip fetch iff every candidate URL (after the same url-dedup rule) is already in ev_store → kept=0 guaranteed → ev_store byte-identical → verdict-identical by construction; ledger transitions unchanged. Measured ceiling ~1-2 min (turns 8-11 fetch = 74s + classify overhead), not the earlier 2-3 min claim. GATE: full-328 A/B + cp4_used==agentic + ~328/329 rendered.
- P3 — Nothing else; do-not-repropose list recorded (R1 batch drain, R3 cheaper decide, turn caps, basket sharding + judge-cap clamp, judge-cap moves, batched judge, bytes cache, section concurrency 3→8, prompt/regen changes — each with its disqualifying invariant).

(3) COMPOSE DEADLOCK — EXACT SAFE FIX: root cause is multiplicative thread oversubscription with no escape valve (sections 3 × workers 16 × ~2 inner-TPE amplification contending on one global BoundedSemaphore; binding entailment acquire at entailment_judge.py:155 has NO timeout; map join and to_thread have no wall; `with TPE` shutdown(wait=True) re-hangs on a wedged worker). The fix that ships is P0: off-loop + workers=1, guard-enforced. Explicitly do NOT add a timeout to the binding acquire (JudgeSlotTimeout → fail-closed ('ENTAILED','judge_error') → dropped sentence → I2 violation) and do NOT ship the judge-cap clamp (collapses to serial at safe knobs; concurrency needs J≈48 which re-opens the 429 storm). Off-loop-only fallback CONFIRMED deadlock-free + verdict-identical: workers=1 → no nested pool, serial byte-identical MAP+REDUCE; peak judge demand 3 ≤ cap 4; 150s POST deadline; empirically, the full 329-basket run completed clean on this exact config (1449.7s, no SIGKILL, faithfulness_pass=true, leaked_cite_ev_tokens=0, agentic held — finish_outline bounced at turn 18 and kept looping).

(4) HONEST CEILING: a faithful, agentic, full-coverage deep render is ~24 min TODAY when nothing goes wrong — that is near the floor. With P2 (next wheel, A/B-gated): ~22-23 min clean; P1 collapses the 30+-min tail toward the clean number. Realistic floor ~20-22 min on current models. Cannot be sped up without sacrificing quality: the serial decide/checklist chain (~391s — it IS the agentic convergence engine; changing it changes the evidence pool → verdict drift), cross-turn fetch overlap (blocked by the decide dependency; intra-search fetch already parallel), the strict_verify/NLI gate at unstarved token budgets under the 4-8 judge cap, drop-driven repair/regen (regen is the coverage-rescue path), and the 329-basket compose+verify work itself — that work is the product, not overhead.
